.

.

.

"It hurts me that Barcelona has moved away from the model that made it great. It hurts me that it has prioritized other things and has not maintained what had made the club so great and above all, so different. If there was something that Barcelona had done differently, it was always the model."

"When Leo gets the ball, there is always a feeling that something is going to happen. Generating that around the world must be something unmatched. And he has generated it every three days for 15 years. We are talking about someone different."

"Leo is a very special person. But not because of what he is as a player but because of how humble and down to Earth he is being what he is. I have not met anyone who is so important in what he does in the history of the sport and who has that capacity to come to talk to you, ask you or ask for an opinion. Normally older people like him don't do it."

When assessing his experience under the command of Pep Guardiola, he said: "I did not have the profile of a midfielder for that team. Pep knew it. But that is the greatest trophy of my career. Having been able to change the way of thinking about it. To me, a coach like Guardiola, possibly the best coach in the last 30 years. Through work, sacrifice and understanding what my role was, I was able to find my place there as a centerback."

He also referred to another Spanish coach for whom he played, Rafa Benítez: "He's a strategist, that's why he always did well in direct playoffs. He's a coach who sees the opponent's weaknesses very well. In the playoffs he had everything done. Of course. I keep talking to him a lot. He has helped me a lot, I value him a lot and whenever I can, I tell him how grateful I am."
